channel.title: CyberNetSec - Industrial Control Systems
channel.description: Latest industrial control systems threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), in partnership with the Department of War, DOE, FBI, and DOS, has published a comprehensive guide for implementing Zero Trust security principles within Operational Technology (OT) environments. The guidance provides a roadmap for sec...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Industrial Control Systems, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Utility Tech Giant Itron Discloses Breach of Internal IT Network
item.description:
Itron, a major American technology provider for energy and water utilities, has disclosed a cybersecurity incident in an SEC filing. The company reported on April 13, 2026, that an unauthorized third party had gained access to a segment of its internal IT systems. Upon discovery, Itron activated its...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Cyberattack, Data Breach, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Cyber Sovereignty and Supply Chain Risk Become Top Concerns for Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
Amid rising geopolitical tensions, the concepts of cyber sovereignty and supply chain security have become paramount for critical infrastructure operators. The recognition that any third-party vendor can be a weak link exploited by adversaries is driving a strategic shift away from cost-based sourci...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Policy and Compliance, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Cyber Sabotage Malware 'fast16' Discovered, Dating Back to 2005
item.description:
Security researchers at SentinelOne have unearthed 'fast16', a previously unknown malware framework from 2005 designed for industrial sabotage. This Lua-based malware predates the infamous Stuxnet worm by at least five years and was engineered to subtly manipulate the output of high-precision calcul...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Malware, Industrial Control Systems, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: NATO's Locked Shields 2026 Cyber Exercise Tests 4,000 Defenders Against 8,000 Live-Fire Attacks
item.description:
The world's largest and most complex live-fire cyber defense exercise, Locked Shields 2026, has concluded in Tallinn, Estonia. Organized by the NATO Cooperative Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence (CCDCOE), the exercise involved over 4,000 participants from 41 NATO and partner nations. The multinatio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK's NCSC Launches 'SilentGlass' Hardware to Block HDMI-Based Cyber Espionage
item.description:
The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) has developed a new hardware device called 'SilentGlass' to protect against cyberattacks transmitted through video display cables. Unveiled at the CYBERUK conference, the plug-and-play device secures HDMI and DisplayPort connections by ensuring only the...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Major US Cement Producer Taps Aria Cybersecurity to Protect Critical Plant Operations
item.description:
Aria Cybersecurity, a business unit of CSPi, has announced an agreement to deploy its AZT PROTECT™ solution to secure the critical operational technology (OT) environments of a major, unnamed US cement producer. The cement industry is considered a high-value target for cyberattacks, including state-...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Cyberattack, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Destructive 'Lotus Wiper' Malware Strikes Venezuelan Energy Sector
item.description:
Researchers from Kaspersky have uncovered 'Lotus Wiper,' a new data-wiping malware used in targeted attacks against Venezuela's energy and utilities sector. The malware is purely destructive, designed to render systems inoperable by erasing recovery mechanisms, overwriting drive contents, and deleti...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'BRIDGE:BREAK' Vulnerabilities Expose Thousands of Serial-to-IP Converters
item.description:
Researchers at Forescout have discovered 22 vulnerabilities, collectively named 'BRIDGE:BREAK,' in popular serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex. These devices, which bridge legacy OT/ICS equipment to modern IP networks, are affected by flaws that could lead to remote code execution, auth...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Industrial Control Systems, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'ZionSiphon' Malware Specifically Targets Israeli Water Infrastructure for Sabotage
item.description:
Security researchers have analyzed ZionSiphon, a new malware strain specifically engineered to target Israeli water infrastructure. The malware, which explicitly references Israel's national water company and major desalination plants, combines data exfiltration and reconnaissance features with capa...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Industrial Control Systems, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →
